titel: The new opportunity to take parental leave has been most frequently used for school meetings.
html:
It appears that you do not have JavaScript enabled in your browser. To use all the features of the Swedish Social Insurance Agency's website, you must enable JavaScript in your browser or upgrade to a browser that supports it.

Your browser is an older version. This means that you may not be able to use all the features on the website. We recommend updating to a newer version.

June 30, 2026

At the beginning of the year, three new situations were introduced in which parents can take parental leave (vab). Of these new situations, parents have most frequently used the option to take parental leave for meetings with school. This is according to the Swedish Social Insurance Agency's follow-up study of the reform.

Since January 1, 2026, there are three new situations in which parents can take parental leave:

The Swedish Social Insurance Agency's follow-up shows that compensation for parental leave in these new situations has been used by nearly 2,500 people during the first half of the year. Considering the total amount of parental leave compensation, parental leave related to these three new situations represents a very small percentage.

"Most commonly, parents have used this new option for meetings with preschool or school," says Alma Wennemo Lanninger, analyst at the Swedish Social Insurance Agency. "The follow-up also shows that most people only take parental leave for part of the day, and that the new options are primarily used for children of school age."

Here's how the new situations have been used:

[Link to press releases]
tldr: Parents who have been granted leave from work to attend school or preschool events with their children have made the most use of this new opportunity.
lattlast:
Starting in early 2026, there are three new situations where parents can receive pay while taking time off from work, a benefit known as "vab." The Swedish Social Insurance Agency (Försäkringskassan), the government agency responsible for administering these regulations, has investigated how these new options are being utilized.

Most of the nearly 2,500 people who have used the new rule have done so to attend meetings with their children at school or preschool. The majority have only been absent for a portion of the day, and it is primarily parents of school-aged children who have taken advantage of this benefit.

Although many are using the new option for school meetings, these payments represent a very small percentage of the total amount paid out for "vab" across Sweden.
